howItWorks.intro: This print bed area calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Usable Area = (Bed Width - 2 × Margin) × (Bed Depth - 2 × Margin)
Usable Volume = Usable Width × Usable Depth × Height ÷ 1000
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a good print bed size for beginners?
faq.answer: 220×220mm is the most popular size for beginners (Ender 3 class). It's large enough for most projects while being affordable. 300×300mm gives more flexibility for larger prints.

faq.question: Why do I need an edge margin?
faq.answer: The outer 3-10mm of most print beds have uneven heating or clips that prevent printing. Keeping a 5mm margin ensures reliable first-layer adhesion.

faq.question: Can I print objects larger than my build volume?
faq.answer: Yes, by splitting the model into pieces and assembling after printing. Design interlocking joints or use adhesive to join sections.

faq.question: Is the Print Bed Area Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard 3D printing and additive manufacturing formulas trusted by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Print Bed Area Calculator?
sections.body: The Print Bed Area Calculator is a free online tool designed for makers and 3D printing enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the 3D printing and additive manufacturing space. By entering your bed width, bed depth, bed height, you get instant results including usable print area, total build volume, usable build volume. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting usable print area right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In 3D printing and additive manufacturing, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your prints with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your bed width and need to find the right usable print area. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Bed Width (X) (mm)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your bed width — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: bed depth, bed height, edge margin. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your usable print area, total build volume, usable build volume. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your bed width before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both usable print area and total build volume — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to optimize your prints.
If you're unsure about your edge margin, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
